behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between evaluate-stage command logic and artifact persistence/routing (slot/key mapping), not a pure local algorithm (`MOCK-MISS` => cross-module write target). `REAL-INFRA`: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. `LLM-DEP`: assertion is artifact destination, not model quality. `MULTI-STEP`: single stage write check. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly checks where a command writes in the artifact system; a unit test of one function can miss wiring mistakes between command and artifact store. Rejected higher levels because behavior text only requires destination correctness for one write.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command with an in-process artifact store (real serialization/path used by app modules), then assert a new `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is unchanged by that write path.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across modules: implement flow must read one artifact and write another (`MOCK-MISS` true for module interaction). `REAL-INFRA`: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess. `LLM-DEP`: behavior is about read/write routing and artifact separation, not judging model output quality. `MULTI-STEP`: within one stage flow, not a full multi-agent chain. Rejected Unit because a single-function test can miss miswired read source or write sink across service boundaries. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text does not require validating model decision quality or multiple real model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact; execute implement flow with deterministic model/tool stubs; assert grounding is consumed (visible in flow inputs/context) and output is persisted to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between deep-dive stage decision logic and artifact write side effects (`MOCK-MISS`: conditional no-write behavior across modules). `REAL-INFRA`: no real OS/network/subprocess required by wording. `LLM-DEP`: assertion is absence of side-effect write, not model output quality. `MULTI-STEP`: single stage condition. Rejected Unit because the bug is specifically an unintended artifact overwrite side effect, which is only visible when stage logic is wired to artifact persistence. Rejected Agentic because behavior text anchors on existing diagnosis presence and write suppression, not quality of LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already contains diagnosis, run deep-dive stage through real stage-to-artifact wiring, assert no write/update occurs to `plan` and existing artifacts remain unchanged.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is cross-module state propagation (artifact identity/run-context lineage) between producer and consumer stages (`MOCK-MISS` true). `REAL-INFRA`: can be reproduced with local in-process store/context; no required real OS/network/subprocess. `LLM-DEP`: correctness is lineage lookup/handoff, not model quality. `MULTI-STEP`: two-stage handoff but not multiple agentic decisions; still integration-level pipeline wiring. Rejected Unit because lineage bugs typically emerge in context/store wiring, not isolated function logic. Rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require validating end-to-end model-driven planning quality, only artifact visibility across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Within one run context, execute grounding-producing stage then implement stage using real context/artifact modules; assert implement resolves the exact prior `grounding` artifact (same lineage/run id) and not stale/foreign artifacts.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step chain and final output reflecting earlier grounding, i.e., sequential agentic handoff correctness across stages (`MULTI-STEP` yes by text). `LLM-DEP`: final plan “reflects grounding” depends on model behavior/content use, which deterministic stubs can hide. Rejected Integration by explicit behavior text requiring full run (`deep-dive -> ... -> implement`) and semantic reflection in final plan, not just plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real multi-stage pipeline in one run context with representative prompt/issue input; assert final `plan` content substantively incorporates facts from earlier `grounding` artifact (traceable markers or rubric), across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration plus artifact-store safety across modules (`MOCK-MISS` true due state interaction). `REAL-INFRA`: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. `LLM-DEP`: validity here is structural pipeline validity and artifact isolation, not subjective model quality. `MULTI-STEP`: single-stage fallback path. Rejected Unit because corruption/no-corruption of neighboring artifacts is a persistence interaction concern. Rejected higher levels because behavior text does not require real external infra or multi-agent semantic evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize run context without `grounding` but with other preexisting artifacts; execute implement flow; assert a valid `plan` artifact is produced, no exceptions, and non-target artifacts are byte-for-byte or version-for-version unchanged.
